channel.title: Home on Georgeos Hardo
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Georgeos Hardo

item.title: Measuring single cell width of bacteria from fluorescence microscopy images
item.description: The difficulties involved in measuring single bacterial widths.

About I’m a PhD student in systems (and synthetic) biology in the Department of Engineering in Somenath Bakshi’s lab at The University of Cambridge. I completed master’s degree in the same university, and my undergraduate degree (B.Eng) in Chemical Engineering at the University of Sheffield. I like DNA, maths, Linux and bacteria. Research One of my major research focusses is in the field of high throughput timelapse microscopy image analysis, where I develop tools to extract information from mother machine experiments.
